element_introduction_order.mastery_context: player learns to manage provinces as core mechanic
managing resources and production across provinces
negotiating with rival factions, forming alliances

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Turn-based pacing allows player-controlled tension management; strategic planning phases interspersed with resolution phases; multiple simultaneous threat vectors create sustained background tension

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction to province governance and basic resource management; single or few rival factions
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Expansion of player influence; introduction of diplomatic, economic, and military complexity; multiple rival factions competing
difficulty_curve.late_game: Full engagement of all game systems (economics, diplomacy, intrigue, warfare); endgame conquest phase
notable_spikes: First major military engagement introduces warfare mechanics
Mid-game transition when multiple factions simultaneously expand
Late-game when remaining rivals deploy coordinated strategies

notable_level_patterns.description: Players can achieve dominance through conquest, diplomacy, economic superiority, or covert action; multiple viable strategies reduce linearity
Multiple rival factions act concurrently; player must balance offense and defense across provinces while managing internal economics
Extended gameplay sessions required to complete empire; reviewer noted game 'will keep players at their keyboards for many a night'

sources_used: Wikipedia - Romance of the Three Kingdoms (video game)
